ops.title: jj commit <fileset> from a subdirectory silently matches nothing (empty described commit) and can snapshot with an empty author; always commit from the repo root
ops.body:
Running `jj commit web/ -m ...` while the shell cwd was `web/` produced "The given paths do not match any file: web/" — jj resolved the fileset relative to cwd (`web/web/`), described the EMPTY working-copy commit with the message, and left the real changes uncommitted. The working copy also got snapshotted with an empty author identity (`<>`) from that cwd.

**Why:** jj filesets are cwd-relative, and the Bash tool's working directory persists between calls — a prior `cd web && npm ...` gate command silently changes where the next jj command runs.

**How to apply:** prefix every jj mutation with `cd <repo root> &&`, and after any fileset commit verify with `jj log -T '... if(empty, "EMPTY", "has-changes")'` that the described commit actually carries the diff. Recovery: `jj squash` the working copy into the described-but-empty parent (fixes both the content and the author, since the parent kept the good identity). Related: [[jj-commit-snapshots-whole-worktree]], [[jj-colocated-use-jj-not-git-stash]].
